task: grasp the door handle, then move the door to the left
raw:
**SCENE ANALYSIS -** A white door is mounted on a wall. A robotic arm is positioned to the left of the door. The door has a handle (a grip-able feature) and a body (the main surface). To move the door, the handle must be grasped to pull it. The door is a rigid object that can be displaced by pulling. **STRATEGY -** First, reach for the door handle to establish contact. Then, grasp the handle to secure a hold. Next, pull the door to the left. The sequence is sequential: reach → grasp → pull. Failure modes include not grasping the handle (no pull) or pulling in the wrong direction. **AFFORDANCE REASONING -** Step 1: reach the door body to position the gripper. Step 2: grasp the door handle (a specific grip-able feature) to enable pulling. Step 3: pull the door to the left (a directional action).

Goal: left_of(door, door_initial_pos); stable(door)
Scene: door, robotic_arm, wall
Step 1: reach(door)
  affordance_region: the body of the white door
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, door, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, door) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(door)
  affordance_region: the handle of the white door
  contact: holding(door) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(door)
Step 3: pull(door -> left)
  affordance_region: the body of the white door
  contact: holding(door) [progress]
  spatial: left_of(door, door_initial_pos) [completion]
  direction: pull_axis(-1, 0, 0) [completion]
  done_when: left_of(door, door_initial_pos)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: the body of the white door
the handle of the white door
the body of the white door
